title: Strictly OOF stacked ensemble with beta calibration and model-disagreement meta-features
summary: Prior work on stacking and beta calibration suggests the next gain may come from combination quality, not a single stronger base learner.
rationale: Prior work on stacking and beta calibration suggests the next gain may come from combination quality, not a single stronger base learner. Beta calibration is more flexible than plain Platt scaling for skewed score distributions, while modern Kaggle stacks often profit from OOF mean/std features that expose model disagreement.
implementation_hint: Build 2-4 intentionally diverse level-1 models, generate predictions strictly OOF with grouped folds, beta-calibrate each model on fold-held-out scores, then train a tiny level-2 learner on calibrated OOF predictions plus `mean`, `std`, `max-min`, and dry/wet regime flags. Sanity-check that raw AUC does not drop after monotone calibration and keep the stack fully fold-pure.
expected_effect: Likely modest but reliable gain on leaderboard stability and public/private consistency, especially if current models are close in CV and make different mistakes around rare stop windows.
risk: Easy to overfit if the meta-learner sees in-fold predictions or too many near-duplicate base models. Calibration may help probability quality more than standalone AUC, so gains can be small.
